item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just so you know, the idea is not terrible, and I like the concept of an SCP based on the Spanish Flu (Or at least that is what I believe it is). It is the execution that is the issue. The story lacks substance and seems to be a standard haunted building. The only nonstandard element is that it involves disease. If you focused more on the disease and the students themselves, then it could maybe have worked. As it is right now, -1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not super realistic that the mass grave would have exactly and exclusively the reported deaths, nor that they all date from the time as a hospital rather than as a residential school. There were *always* more deaths then were reported, usually a hugely disturbing amount of them. Not surprising that none would be reported, but certainly there would have been deaths in that time frame.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
All of the subjects were issued with Class-C amnestics and placed under surveillance.

This is oddly terrifying to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's a great start here, but with the mention of paperwork that's never linked and a POI without any interview logs whatsoever… it feels like what it is, a start. No vote for now until I see where this goes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh yell heah, gotta pop on the detective cap for this one. If I manage to figure it out, will update this comment.
